# Developer How-To & Cheatsheet

This document is intended specifically for developers working on the dashboard architecture. It provides practical snippets for troubleshooting, running manual tests, and environment setup. 

For conceptual knowledge or user-facing instructions, please see the [Additional Utilities](additional_utilities.qmd) document.

## 1. Setting up the Terminal Environment
Before running any manual python scripts or loaders from the command line, you must activate the virtual environment and ensure your paths are set correctly.

```bash
# 1. Define the root of the Git repository
ROOT="/home/evezeyl/Documents/Insync/gdrive/OBSWORK/20_GITS/SPARMVET_VIZ"

# 2. Activate the virtual environment
source $ROOT/.venv/bin/activate

# 3. Add the project root to the PYTHONPATH so modules like `libs.utils` resolve correctly
export PYTHONPATH=$ROOT:$PYTHONPATH
```

## 2. Testing the Configuration Loader

When developing new YAML features (like the `!include` strategy), you can test the Python `ConfigManager` directly without booting up the entire Shiny dashboard.

Create or run the test script located alongside the module (e.g. `libs/utils/tests/test_config_loader.py`) with the environment activated:

```python
{{< include ../../libs/utils/tests/test_config_loader.py >}}
```

Run it:
```bash
python3 libs/utils/tests/test_config_loader.py
```

## 3. Running Helper Scripts Manually

As developers, you will frequently use the helper scripts located in `assets/scripts/` to generate test data and validate the wrangling logic.

Please refer to the [Additional Utilities](additional_utilities.qmd) documentation for the full list of arguments for each script.

### Generating Test Data
To inject edge cases (duplicates, mismatches, and nulls) for testing the ingestion layer:

```bash
SCRIPT_DIR="$ROOT/assets/scripts"
DATA_IN_DIR="assets/test_data/raw"

python3 $SCRIPT_DIR/create_test_data.py \
  --data_file $DATA_IN_DIR/raw_pipeline_output.tsv \
  --primary_key_data sample_id \
  --mismatches --duplicates --missing_values 
```

### Auto-Scaffolding a Modular Manifest

To test the Native Modular YAML scaffolding (`!include`), point the manifest generator at a directory of TSV files. It will automatically build the `Master File` and the folder containing the separate schema fragments.

```bash
SCRIPT_DIR="$ROOT/assets/scripts"
DUMMYDATA_DIR="assets/test_data/my_extracted_pipeline"

python3 $SCRIPT_DIR/create_manifest.py \
  --data_dir $DUMMYDATA_DIR \
  --primary_key_data sample_id \
  --out_file config/manifests/pipelines/My_New_Test.yaml
```

*(Remember to manually align the generated `target_dataset` in the Plotly config block after generation!)*
